title: A small park behind a hospital in Sofia
summary: A short description of a hidden park you found by accident in a foreign city.
topic: A small park behind a hospital in Sofia
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a small, hidden park, garden, or quiet place you discovered by accident abroad. Describe it and what you do there. Mix past simple (how you found it) with present simple (what it is like now).
hintStarters: I found the park by accident one afternoon when I was looking for…
It sits behind a large grey hospital, hidden by…
Now I go there every Saturday morning to…
sampleText: I found the park by accident one cold March afternoon when I was looking for a pharmacy in central Sofia. It sits behind a large grey hospital on Sveti Georgi Sofiyski street, completely hidden by a wall and three tall chestnut trees. From the main road you cannot see a single leaf. Inside, there are only six benches, one small stone fountain, and an old man who sweeps the path every morning with a long straw broom. Now I go there every Saturday morning to read for an hour. The benches are usually empty before nine. Sometimes a young nurse in green scrubs sits at the other end of mine and eats a sandwich without saying anything. We have never spoken, but we always nod. The fountain is broken and full of dry leaves, but somehow that makes the park better, not worse. I write a few lines in my notebook before I leave. I have lived in Sofia for almost two years now, and I have never told anyone about this park, not even my closest friend. Some places stay your places only because you do not share them too quickly.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi tìm thấy công viên một cách tình cờ

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Bây giờ tôi tới đó mỗi sáng thứ Bảy để đọc sách một tiếng

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present perfect for life experience to date
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I have lived in Sofia for almost two years now' và 'We have never spoken' dùng present perfect cho khoảng thời gian/kinh nghiệm nối từ quá khứ đến hiện tại.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I have lived in Sofia for almost two years now' and 'We have never spoken' use present perfect for spans of time or experiences connecting past to present.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous in 'when'-clause
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'when I was looking for a pharmacy' dùng past continuous mô tả việc đang diễn ra lúc đó; mệnh đề chính ở past simple ('I found').
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'When I was looking for a pharmacy' uses past continuous for the ongoing activity; the main clause is in past simple ('I found').

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi tìm thấy công viên một cách tình cờ vào một chiều tháng Ba lạnh khi tôi đang tìm một hiệu thuốc ở trung tâm Sofia. Nó nằm phía sau một bệnh viện lớn màu xám trên phố Sveti Georgi Sofiyski, bị che khuất hoàn toàn bởi một bức tường và ba cây dẻ cao. Từ đường chính bạn không thể nhìn thấy một chiếc lá nào. Bên trong, chỉ có sáu chiếc ghế dài, một đài phun nước đá nhỏ, và một ông cụ quét lối đi mỗi sáng bằng một chổi rơm dài. Bây giờ tôi tới đó mỗi sáng thứ Bảy để đọc sách một tiếng. Các ghế dài thường trống trước chín giờ. Đôi khi một y tá trẻ mặc đồ y tế màu xanh ngồi ở đầu kia ghế của tôi và ăn một chiếc sandwich mà không nói gì. Chúng tôi chưa bao giờ nói chuyện, nhưng luôn gật đầu chào. Đài phun nước bị hỏng và đầy lá khô, nhưng điều đó bằng cách nào đó lại khiến công viên đẹp hơn, không tệ hơn. Tôi viết vài dòng trong sổ tay trước khi rời đi. Tôi đã sống ở Sofia gần hai năm nay, và tôi chưa kể về công viên này với ai, ngay cả người bạn thân nhất của tôi. Có những nơi vẫn còn là nơi của bạn chỉ vì bạn không chia sẻ chúng quá nhanh.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple sentence about discovery.
options: I found the park by accident one cold March afternoon.
I find the park by accident one cold March afternoon.
I have found the park by accident one cold March afternoon.
I am finding the park by accident one cold March afternoon.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'found' for a finished past event with a definite time.

options: when I look for a pharmacy
when I was looking for a pharmacy
when I have looked for a pharmacy
when I am looking for a pharmacy
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was looking' for what was happening when the main event occurred.

options: very loud
very bright
kept out of sight
newly built
an old man which sweeps the path every morning
an old man what sweeps the path every morning
an old man whose sweeps the path every morning
an old man who sweeps the path every morning
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Who' for people; present simple 'sweeps' for habitual action.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct present perfect sentence.
options: I have lived in Sofia for almost two years now.
I am living in Sofia since almost two years.
I live in Sofia for almost two years now.
I had lived in Sofia for almost two years now.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect + 'for + duration' for time from past to now.

options: We have never speak.
We have never spoken.
We never have spoken.
We did never spoke.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Have + never + past participle' for an experience that has not happened.

options: a bright light
the colour of a leaf
an area protected from direct sun
a kind of small bird
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Shade' = a darker area sheltered from sunlight.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence about a habit.
options: I am going there every Saturday morning to read for an hour.
I have gone there every Saturday morning to read for an hour.
I went there every Saturday morning to read for an hour.
I go there every Saturday morning to read for an hour.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple for a current habit; infinitive 'to read' for purpose.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'full of'.
options: The fountain is broken and full of dry leaves.
The fountain is broken and full from dry leaves.
The fountain is broken and full with dry leaves.
The fountain is broken and full at dry leaves.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Full of + noun' is the natural collocation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence about non-sharing.
options: I have never tell anyone about this park.
I have never told anyone about this park.
I am never told anyone about this park.
I never have tell anyone about this park.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect 'have + told' (past participle); 'never' before main verb.

options: Some places stay your places only because you do not share it too quickly.
Some places stays your places only because you do not share them too quickly.
Some places stay your places only because you do not share them too quickly.
Some places stay yours only because you do not shared them too quickly.
mcQuestions.explanation: Plural agreement 'places stay'; 'them' refers back to plural; present simple 'share' after 'do not'.
mcQuestions.hint: Plural noun + plural verb; plural pronoun.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Do quiet places stay quiet because they are hidden, or because the people who find them choose not to share them?
